title: Greedy Colouring Algorithm
description: Greedy colouring assigns colours to vertices in a given order, using smallest available colour
code:
GREEDY_COLOURING(G, order[])
BEGIN
 FOR i = 1 to n DO
 colour[i] = 0 // Initialize all vertices as uncoloured
 
 FOR i = 1 to n DO
 v = order[i]
 used[1..n] = FALSE // Colours used by neighbours
 
 FOR each neighbour u of v DO
 IF colour[u] ≠ 0 THEN
 used[colour[u]] = TRUE
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Assign smallest available colour
 c = 1
 WHILE used[c] = TRUE DO
 c = c + 1
 END WHILE
 
 colour[v] = c
 END FOR
 
 RETURN colour
END
explanation: The algorithm processes vertices in the given order. For each vertex, it marks colours used by already-coloured neighbours, then assigns the smallest unused colour. Time complexity: O(n + m) where n vertices, m edges. Space: O(n). Worst-case uses Δ(G)+1 colours, best case uses χ(G).

title: Chromatic Polynomial Calculation
description: Recursive algorithm using deletion-contraction for chromatic polynomial
code:
CHROMATIC_POLYNOMIAL(G, k)
BEGIN
 IF |V(G)| = 0 THEN
 RETURN 1 // Empty graph: 1 way to colour
 END IF
 
 IF |V(G)| = 1 THEN
 RETURN k // Single vertex: k colours available
 END IF
 
 // Choose an edge e = (u, v)
 e = ANY_EDGE(G)
 
 // G-e: Graph with edge e deleted
 G1 = DELETE_EDGE(G, e)
 
 // G/e: Graph with edge e contracted (u and v merged)
 G2 = CONTRACT_EDGE(G, e)
 
 // Deletion-Contraction recurrence
 RETURN CHROMATIC_POLYNOMIAL(G1, k) - CHROMATIC_POLYNOMIAL(G2, k)
END
explanation: Uses the deletion-contraction recurrence: P(G, k) = P(G-e, k) - P(G/e, k). The chromatic polynomial counts all proper k-colourings. Base cases: empty graph has 1 colouring, single vertex has k colourings. Time is exponential in worst case.

title: Check if Graph is k-Colourable
description: Backtracking algorithm to check if a graph can be properly coloured with k colours
code:
IS_K_COLOURABLE(G, k)
BEGIN
 colour[1..n] = 0 // 0 means uncoloured
 
 RETURN BACKTRACK(1)
 
 BACKTRACK(v)
 BEGIN
 IF v > n THEN
 RETURN TRUE // All vertices coloured
 END IF
 
 FOR c = 1 to k DO
 IF SAFE_TO_COLOUR(v, c) THEN
 colour[v] = c
 IF BACKTRACK(v+1) THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 colour[v] = 0 // Backtrack
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN FALSE
 END
 
 SAFE_TO_COLOUR(v, c)
 BEGIN
 FOR each neighbour u of v DO
 IF colour[u] = c THEN
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN TRUE
 END
END
explanation: Backtracking tries all possible colour assignments. To find chromatic number, try k = 1, 2, 3... until colouring succeeds. For complete graphs, must try all k colours. Time is O(k^n) in worst case - exponential but works for small graphs.
